decisions.decision: Use session.generate_reply(instructions=...) not user_input=... for enforcement
decisions.rationale: Context7 confirmed instructions= does NOT pollute chat history whereas user_input= adds a synthetic user message. Cleaner log, same bypass of prompt cache.
Previous system fought races between LLM, TTS, data channel, teardown. New model: modal is user-confirmed (eliminates race), watchdog only fires if frontend is presumed dead.

decisions.decision: Remove extension flow entirely in Tier 0 (can re-add as modal button later)
decisions.rationale: extend_interview tool + state extension tracking was load-bearing fragile code that would collide with new pending_finalize staging. Defer to Tier 2 UX work.

decisions.decision: Backend agent_state_changed handler delivers pending_finalize on speaking→listening transition
decisions.rationale: Makes modal appear in the silence AFTER TTS playout completes, not during. 20s timeout fallback if transition never fires. Context7 confirmed event name + payload structure.

decisions.decision: Per-speaker liveInterim slot (not append) for transcript display
decisions.rationale: Deepgram emits interim segments with rotating IDs and cumulative text. Previous fix skipped interims entirely (fixed duplication, broke live display). Slot replaces on each interim, commits on final.

Multiple trigger paths (agent-delivered, frontend fallback timer, user-early-click) all funnel through a single requestModal() that's guarded by state.kind === 'idle'. First trigger wins. Prevents race conditions across paths.

decisions.decision: Worktree-based deploy (.claude/worktrees/deploy-6cca276) for the first Vercel attempt
decisions.rationale: Kept master free for active Tier 0+1 refactor work. Worktree branch (detached HEAD at 6cca276 + 815bee4) isolated the build-fix patches from master's refactor.

decisions.decision: Playwright CLI (not MCP) with tests/e2e/ directory and webServer auto-start
decisions.rationale: User explicit: CI-ready tests that can run from terminal without interactive browser. Voice-pipeline tests marked fixme with unblock strategies.
Scoped by inviteToken (known at both interview-room.tsx writer and interview-flow-wrapper.tsx reader levels). 10-minute TTL prevents stale restoration.

decisions.decision: closing_reason enum: natural | time_up | user_requested | fallback | watchdog
decisions.rationale: Covers all 5 closing paths. Enforcement maps internal reason to DB enum (time_limit_90pct / idle_timeout → 'time_up'). LLM-initiated close without enforcement → 'natural'. Watchdog branch → 'watchdog'. Frontend timer fallback reserved as 'fallback' (not currently set from backend side).

next_action: Run `railway login` in a terminal, then /gsd-resume-work. Resume will relaunch the Railway deploy agent with authentication available. First concrete step in the new session: apply Supabase migration 006 via dashboard (takes 30s), then wait for Railway deploy to complete, then manual browser smoke test on https://interview-kkhp1b9a2-not-so-software.vercel.app/interview/9aefb39a-c5ab-4a0e-95c8-0e00022fb83f
context_notes: This session shipped a massive Tier 0 + Tier 1 refactor of the voice interview closing flow. 14 commits on master (Waves 1.1-1.7, 2.1-2.4, plus docs, Playwright scaffold, and a checkpoint). Zero TypeScript errors, 22/22 Python unit tests pass. The Vercel preview is live and serving HTTP 200 responses from the pre-refactor baseline (815bee4 on worktree — NOT the current master). Master still needs its first production build verification. Three background subagents ran in parallel this session: Vercel env vars (success), Railway deploy (blocked on login), Playwright scaffold (success). The CURRENT-SYSTEM.md documentation at .planning/phases/03-voice-interview/ is the canonical reference for how the system currently works — a resuming agent should read it FIRST before touching any interview code.
